chapter: A Recap of Linear Models & Basic Plots (optional)
heading: A.1 Linear Models

chapter: A Recap of Linear Models & Basic Plots (optional)
heading: A.1.1 Linear Regression Models
text:
Linear regression models commonly used type linear model, particularly useful response predictor variables continuous. use linear regression models quantify relationship two variables can identify trends make predictions data.Linear regression models fit straight line relationship response variable continuous explanatory variable, can shown equation:
(insert equation linear regression model)\[
y = mx + c
\]code linear regression model, can use following syntax:Tip: naming models objects R, try use specific names know object explicitly. Using short names like model1, model2, etc. can get confusing.Frequent Mistake:
R handle spaces object names. options alternative syntax, recommend using camelCase letters capitalised indicate new words (e.g. specificModelName), using underscore connect words (e.g. specific_model_name).using camelCase, remember R case sensitive - object named specificModelName, call specificmodelname, R show error:view output linear regression model, can use summary() function:show lot information (useful familiarise ), workshop, primarily interested Coefficients table.table one row coefficient model - two rows simple linear regression model: Intercept Slope.
Intercept estimate represents predicted value response variable explanatory variable zero.
Slope () estimate proportional correlation coefficient response variable explanatory variable, scaled ratio standard deviations. Put simply, estimate represents strength (value) direction (positive negative) relationship response explanatory variables.Coefficient table also shows details t-test, tells us whether slope intercept significantly different zero.Std. Error - far observations sit regression line.t value - estimate divided standard error.Pr(>|t|) - p-value, used inform probability observing result true coefficient zero.Tip:
plot line best fit, reflects mean, variance, correlations dataset. Knowledge model coefficients help better understand plots visualisations later workshop.
code:
specificModelName <- lm(responseVariable ~ explanatoryVariable, data = yourDataset)#> Error: object ‘specificmodelname’ not found.
summary(specificModelName)

chapter: A Recap of Linear Models & Basic Plots (optional)
heading: A.1.2 ANOVA Models
text:
happens explanatory variable categorical, rather continuous? ANOVA models come , ANalysis VAriance across means different groups categories. ANOVA useful type linear model, particularly response variable continuous, explanatory variable categorical.Coding ANOVA essentially linear regression model - still use lm() function fit relationship response explanatory variable. However, now explanatory variable categorical, slope represents difference group (category) means., can use summary() function check model output view Coefficients table (laid way linear regression model output).Despite looking , differences ANOVA output:
Intercept mean first category.
Slope difference mean first category (named) category.summary() function can used assess differences specific categories, anova() function can tell us whether categorical explanatory variable whole significant effect response variable.output anova() function also provides table, lists following statistics:Df - refers number categories observations.Sum Sq & Mean Sq - measures variance within categories.F-value - ratio explained variance unexplained variance (higher F-value indicates categorical variable significant effect).Pr(>F) - p-value, used inform probability observing F-value true difference categories.Tip:Use summary() interested category means differences explanatory variable.Use anova() interested overall significance explanatory variable response variable.interested linear models, can find resources :Linear Models: Multiple explanatory variablesLinear Models: Multiple variables interactionsThis additional content optional, necessary complete workshop.
code:
specificModelName <- lm(continuousResponse ~ categoricalExplanatory, data = yourDataset)
summary(specificModelName)
anova(specificModelName)

heading: A.2.1 Scatter Plots
text:
scatter plot can used want visualise relationship two continuous variables.Plotting raw data scatter plot allows identify trends data, including direction strength relationship variables. can also spot outlier observations deviate general pattern, shape data can help decide whether linear non-linear model appropriate dataset.However, large dataset, points scatter plot can overlap much plot becomes messy difficult interpret.Walkthrough Task

See Reading Resources raw versions data files used workshop. expected use , seeing raw format might help better understand data working .Although data workshop tidied , good practice check data. Let’s assess type data working using … function:

just press plot() without describing specific variables dataset, end messy overwhelming visualisation. Use $ specify specific variables want compare.

chapter: A Recap of Linear Models & Basic Plots (optional)
heading: A.2.2 Histograms
text:
Histograms used visualise distribution single continuous variable. done splitting range values intervals, called bins, calculating frequency data points sit interval.Histograms can particularly useful check whether data normally distributed skewed, view variance (spread) data, identify gaps data may suggest issues data collection.Histograms show us relationship variables. However, can plot two histograms two variables want compare can assess distributions two variables.Walkthrough Task
